query: glitch art pixel sorting digital corruption datamoshing
message: sub:digital_artifacts/glitch — anti_aesthetic — pixel sorting, color shifts, datamosh, digital corruption

response.objective_caption: Vertical ridges and grooves in warm amber, gold, and orange tones densely fill the frame, running from top to bottom. A soft shadow with branching, finger-like extensions falls across the right side of the ridged surface, reaching toward a deep teal and black area in the lower right corner. Minute bright highlights are scattered across the textured surface, while cool blue-green tones emerge within the recessed channels and shaded sections.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: Heavy analog film grain blankets an abstract, tightly cropped view of vertical amber and gold striations, rendering the subject ambiguous and secondary to tactile texture, color fields, and rhythmic vertical lines. A soft, branching shadow spreads across the right side toward a crushed teal-black corner. The image abandons representational clarity in favor of dense noise and archival, lo-fi materiality.
